item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A. F.'s wife did it.

Excellent writing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh wait uuuuuuuhhhhh… feck. Lemme change the wording.

E: There we go. That's vague enough to work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a counterpoint, if you work in finance, and deal with financial systems you're generally required to take two uninterrupted weeks off a year.

This is to prevent embezzlement. Anything you're doing to steal money won't survive two weeks of someone else doing your job.

So, sometimes mandatory leave is vacation.

item.title: Re: @Mafia Kirby
item.content:encoded:
Pretty much this. Now, this isnt to say they don't get other vacation times. it's just… this is the one where they're allowed to leave Site-45.

What happens in Vegas, amiright?

item.title: @Mafia Kirby
item.content:encoded: I believe their mandatory time off is their vacation time. You can't just have people walking around Vegas with eldritch rabbit masks stuck to their faces! Or maybe you can. Ethics committee, where you at?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just noticed something about this otherwise great article that bugged me. The document was written in the 1940s, yet a footnote references DNA, which wasn't discovered until the 1950s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, their mandatory time off comes out of their vacation time? Jeez, foundation IS evil.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

To link to this article's sequel, I propose the following edit:

Dr. Hayward's testimony has matched those of other SCP-1903 personnel, who have similarly hypothesized that Mr. A███ Donnar was a part of this shared hallucination.

to

Dr. Hayward's testimony has matched those of other SCP-1903 personnel, who have similarly hypothesized that Mr. A███ Donnar was a part of this shared hallucination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of my favourite infohazards. Knowing the ritual makes you a victim of the ritual.

I'm gonna go out on a limb here and guess that the [REDACTED] in her eye was semen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this article gave me a phobia that doesn't exist, and that I can't describe.

+1 for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap, this was amazing. Coming over from SCP-2999 but this definitely enriched the backstory and overall creep factor for me. Thank you for writing this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

Jackie is another incarnation of the Scholar, Clovis.

I think that is on my chart, but maybe not. Probably past time to try to update it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm wondering how this is related to SCP-2746:

+ Spoilers
– hide block

Jackie is a reincarnation of one of the inhabitants of SCP-2746, is suffering from the curse placed on that entity, and the curse is transmissible/contagious through knowledge?
Yeah boy~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She has the same name as me +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a long shot,and Dr. Nietzsche seems to have gotten most of the references, but I'd been wondering if this could have something to do with the "Scarlet Woman" as well.
A solid puzzle-box of a skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just discoved Pitch Haven Verse, and this is definitely my favorite stand alone one. Works amazingly divorced from the other works, and gets even better when you read the others. Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*dumps all the +1s on Fantem*

Just take them! All of them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two reads I'm still not sure exactly what's going on. Probably just the insomnia. I didn't even get that Jackie used to be a prostitute until reading the comments.
Anyway fantastic choice of the first pic. I put off reading this just because it terrified me. I also like that someone was inspired enough to send you a doll based on your creation.
No vote for now but I'm strongly inclined to upvote once it all clicks in my head.

On a second read I'm blown away by the narrative I've seen so far. I look forward to finishing the Pitch Haven canon to see how everything links together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good catch; got it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From REVISION-2:
"█████ █████'s female uniform included a pair of novelty animal ears, and a white mask. Both parts would be paired so that the wearer would resemble either a cat, fox, wolf, or rat."

Seems to be missing rabbits.

item.title: Re: So I just got a birthday present, and...
item.content:encoded:
Happy birthday.

Also, contain this doll. Immediately. It's too cute

+1

item.title: Re: So I just got a birthday present, and...
item.content:encoded: D'awww this is adorably disturbing. :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's even creepier in real life. I was like "oh yeah, I'll check this out and JESUS CHRIST!" Well done.

item.title: So I just got a birthday present, and...
item.content:encoded:
[+] Show gift
[-] Why would you hide gift?
…I think I am in love.

This was made by a very good friend of mine back at home. I don't know if she wants her name up here, so I'll ask next time I see her. Had to show y'all, but with no idea where to put it, I thought this'd be a good a place as any.

I feel honor-bound to make this a skip, and it shall be so as soon as I have ideas germinating.
Happy Birthday.

I just saw how high this got, and just wanted to thank you all and say how huge a milestone this is for me. Coming this close the 100 mark is probably one of the best birthday gifts anyone could give.

It's been an honor working with you all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My obsession knows no bounds, but what can I say? Something dawned on me when I just re-read the last interview. Everyone turns their heads to look at him, like they've done something wrong. I looked back at the masks, and noticed something: Jackie does the 'shush' thing, and the 'lip' portions of the masks…they're all closed. And they don't look…right.

Like, say, they're wired shut.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Paranoia fuel at its finest, fantastic work Fantem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First time reading this, and I only knew of it from the link in the white rabbit/black cat draft… I was kind of underwhelmed at first until I read about the Valhalla explanation. Really, really good job here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha! Good ol' fashion creep! +1 wish I could do it more. Very good. Love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a lovely costume, though, I would've added some press-on nails.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two people? Wow… Do you have any pics? :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it's really good quality; she even painted her hands and feet for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hot fucking damn that's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … I have been brought to tears of joy. That's how happy I am… Thank you for sharing this with me. You've made my week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuck. Hats off to you, Fantem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's funny, because I actually made a costume based on 1903 as well. I went and fucked with some people, got some good reactions.
Fantem, I second Ihp in saying that you should feel proud. You have managed to creep me out. I do not scare easily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I was browsing the SCP subreddit, and found that someone made a Halloween costume based on this SCP. Credit for the images goes to /u/astraelly, and the full thread is here.

Fantem, feel proud of yourself. You have someone cosplaying as your skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know, but it's better than knowing. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're slowly driving me insane with not-knowing. I hope you know that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't tell you how wrong or right you were. I've been about as truthful as the devil when it came to the details of my recent SCPs for a reason. You aren't supposed to know it in its entirety yet.

I am however, very impressed with the information you've managed to glean from this part of the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds reasonable to me.

-cough-

You never did tell me how close I was to being right here. It is eating away at me to know, damn it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm thinking of saying that personnel with 1903 clearance would only have to remain inside of Site 45, rather than just one floor. My reasoning is that foundation employees have seen weirder things, and would be informed of 1903's basic effect on people before their arrival to Site 45, coupled with the fact that remaining on one floor, even if it's on the larger side, would be somewhat more traumatizing than it needs to be.

Would anyone have any objections/opinions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because it's describing the nature of the SCP, and it would be inefficient to isolate just one person to research it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1903 personnel are to remain on the 9th floor of Site 45 indefinitely. There must be at least fifteen personnel with SCP-1903 status.

When a subject learns of A██████ F████'s name, A██████ F████'s specific actions towards SCP-1903, and the nature of SCP-1903's commissions for A██████ F████ prior to containment, that subject will experience symptoms similar to those affecting SCP-1903 while under containment.

Why do any personnel need to know this? What's the point in having 15 people suffer through all that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got it. Glad You liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, lordy. Interesting backstory, but what I liked the most was the sudden infohazard. Bam. The revisions reminded me of 106 and they held my attention, thinking what whammy would be to come at the end. Also, kudos for using a self-made picture!

Might've stumbled upon a typo though.

Two to three days later, the blood will begin to seep through the pores in the subject's hands, feet, and gums.

I believe the "the" in "the blood" shouldn't be there, as no mention of the mercury-blood mix isn't made for quite a while before the paragraph.

item.title: Re: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded:
All ends well! That is a fantastic response.

You are awesome! Can't wait for your next one.

item.title: Re: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded:
I have received the reply:

Ooooohhh, omg! I loved it!!!!

Well, my english is not that good, so I may not have understood everything, but I still loved it!! hahaha =D

I really like the way you contextualized the picture in the story!!
And I'm so glad with the comments I was reading! Specially those who are scared! hahahaha

Thank you so much, and thanks to everyone who liked it!!

To which I replied:

I'm really glad you liked it! I can't tell you how truly satisfying it was to find that the original artist liked my interpretation.

Take care! =3

Fantem

All is well, and everyone is happy. =3

item.title: Re: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded: That sounds like a perfect approach! Best of luck that they don't mind. It really helps make the article.

item.title: Re: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded:
Because DeviantArt has some kind of weird spam blocker, I had to send her a private note. For the record, here's what it said:

Subject: Story

Hi, I'm a writer. I recently wrote an article for the SCP Foundation featuring your IMG_1314 as the main image.

I apologize for not notifying you first, but I was not aware it had an actual owner at the time. It just sort of appeared on my phone without my knowledge, and when I saw it, my first reaction was to write a story about it. To fit with the setting I was writing in, I had to edit the photo a tiny bit (making it monochrome, and adding an old photo texture). I made sure to attribute you, but if you do not wish me to use the photo, then I'll take it down.

If you wish to see the story, here's the link: www.scp-wiki.net/scp-1903.

item.title: Re: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff! I doubt it will be a problem, but usually important to try when you manage locate the source.

It IS a lovely picture, too. <3

item.title: Re: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded: No. I am in the process of making an account and notifying her though.

item.title: Re: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded: Permission received?

item.title: I have found the source!
item.content:encoded:
After much digging, I have finally found the original creator of the first picture! I have never worked so hard to find a DeviantArt page in my life.

Credit goes to photoutopia for this b-e-a-utiful first picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, I think I know how the hands in the first picture were made like that.
It could simply be rubber gloves partially filled with a liquid at the tips and with a little bit of slack, giving it the look of claws.

Of course, I could be wrong, just WMG here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have managed to out-ophite ophite. +1

+1
+1
+1
+1
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Donnar didn't come to her. A.F did. He was cheating on his wife with Oster, the goddess of fertility. Donnar/Thor is also a God of Fertility..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And that's why nobody ever came back except to her except for 'Donner' himself?
